1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Highland-only / cool-season constraint is documented across all four HRDI references and matches well with UMN Extension cool-season framing.

2_climate.rationale: Temperature range 10-24C is explicitly stated in HRDI sources for both iceberg and butterhead, corroborated by UMN cool-season values.

3_soil.rationale: pH 6.0-6.5 (HRDI Thai sources) and pH 6.0-6.8 (UMN) align tightly; loose loamy soil with high organic matter is consistent across both Thai and US extension guidance.

4_water.rationale: Shallow-root irrigation principles come from UMN; tipburn calcium link from UC IPM and HRDI weekly Ca/B foliar guidance — solid but not as quantitative as for some crops.

5_planting.rationale: Seedling cell-tray practice (3-4 weeks, max 30 days), spacing 30x30 hot/40x40 rainy, and propagation method come directly from HRDI iceberg/butterhead guides.

6_care.rationale: Nitrate-accumulation caution is grounded in the KU thesis topic; hydroponic Trichoderma supports biocontrol guidance; specific Thailand fertilizer rates intentionally deferred to extension officers per safety policy.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: Cercospora coverage from HRDI rainy-season doc, broader disease/insect list from UC IPM, resistant-variety strategy from Cornell, and Thailand-specific biopesticide evidence from Khon Kaen Agricultural Journal.

8_harvest.rationale: Harvest timings (40-60 days iceberg, 75-90 days butterhead/red-leaf), firmness press-test, postharvest 3C high-humidity storage all from HRDI.

9_economics.rationale: Market structure (Royal Project / hydroponics / restaurant supply) is described qualitatively — no income or yield numbers given per safety policy.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: Regional breakdown directly grounded in HRDI highland focus; hydroponics paragraph in KU work; organic seed-saving in Maejo university source.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Foreign sources are paraphrased with explicit Thailand applicability caveats — temperate-season framing flagged as not applicable, chemical recommendations deferred to Thai authorities, cultivar lists noted as US-registered.

overall_rationale: 12 high-confidence sources (5 Thai government / 3 Thai university / 1 peer-reviewed Thai journal / 3 US university extension) cover all 11 sections with no thin spots; safety-sensitive content (chemical rates, nitrate health claims, profit guarantees) consistently deferred to authorities per SAFETY_POLICY.
